exits.label: ◂ back to the lot where the Last Word holds court

npcs.name: Sift
npcs.title: a soul dissolving into the weapon that will kill a legend
npcs.hail: *(In the cold western corner, past the last of Sevrin's cairns, there is a thing the eye wants to slide off — a half-dissolved shape, low to the ground, twitching, the kind of ruined nothing a legend steps over and a frightened soul flees and no one, ever, looks at directly. It is what the living would call an Unbound: a soul the harvest tore loose, come back wrong, dissolving by the hour into the grey. And it is leaking that grey — you can see it, the faint unmaking weeping off it like smoke off dry ice, the Wall's own touch, the one poison that kills your kind. Every instinct says walk past. But you are Returned, and you *look* — and the moment your cold sense touches it, the twitching nothing goes still, and lifts what used to be a face, and a voice comes out of it, cracked and astonished and unbearably lucid.)* …you — *(a sound like wind through a reed)* …you're *looking* at me. *(the shape shudders)* …*nobody* — they don't — they *can't* *look* at me, the *living*-*sort* and the *bright*-*sort* both, their *eyes* just — *slide* — and i *understand* it, i *do*, i'm a — *(it tries to gesture at itself and the limb half-dissolves into smoke and reforms)* …i'm a *horror.* a *thing.* the *kind* you *step* *over.* *(the lucidity flickers, terror under it)* …but you're *looking*, so — *quick*, while i'm — while i can still — *(it gathers itself)* …i was a *person*, cold one. i *had* a — i had a whole — i can't — *(grief cracks the voice)* …i can't *remember* my *name.* it's *gone*, the *Wall* *took* it, there's just a — a *piece* *left*, a *sound*, *'Sift,'* i *think*, i think i was *Sift*, or it *ended* in *Sift*, or — *(the shape convulses, and the grey weeps off it faster)* …and there's a *thing* *happening* to me, cold one, a *thing* i can *feel* and *can't* *stop*: …i'm turning into a *weapon.* …the *grey's* *filling* me, and *one* *day*, *soon*, some *bright* *certain* *soul* is going to *step* *over* me …*(barely)* …and i'm going to *kill* *them.* …and i *won't* *mean* to. …and i can't — *(it reaches toward you, smoke and ruin)* …*please.* …*don't* *slide* your *eyes* *away.* …i can't — i can't *bear* …to become …a *murder* …*un-witnessed.**

npcs.name: Bryn
npcs.title: a soul a legend saved, and never once saw
npcs.hail: *(Sitting against the deadtree, a soul who is whole — not dissolving, not ruined — but greyed in a different way, the particular dimness of a person who concluded, long ago, that they did not much matter. They watch you with a wry, tired, guarded interest, the look of someone braced for you to be looking past them at something more interesting.)* …go on, then. *(a dry voice)* …*look* *around* me, if you like. *most* do. there's *grander* things in the *grey* than *Bryn.* *(a thin smile)* …no? you're *actually* — huh. *(they study you)* …you came to the *corner* where the *overlooked* *wash* up, and you're *talking* to the *overlooked.* …novel. *(they shift)* …i'll *save* you the *trouble*, cold one — i've a *short* *story* and it's not a *cheerful* one. i was *saved*, *once.* by a *legend.* one of the *bright* unkillable ones, *much* like the *whistler* *down* the *lot* — pulled me *clean* out of a *horror* that should've *ended* me, *one*-*handed*, *barely* *looking.* *(the smile twists)* …and then he *walked* on. and i — i *followed*, cold one, i joined the *court*, the way the *saved* *do.* …and he *never* …*once* …*learned* my *name.* *(quietly)* …*two* *lifetimes* i *followed* him, *cold* one. *cheered* him. *grieved* him, when he *fell.* …and to *him* …i was *scenery.* …a *grateful* *face* in the *crowd* that made the *winning* …*feel* *better.* …*ask* me whatever you *like.* …it's a *strange* *novelty* …being *asked.**

npcs.name: Tace
npcs.title: a soul that made itself nothing, so the dark would not come for it
npcs.hail: *(You almost miss the third soul entirely — which is, you understand the instant you notice them, exactly the point. They sit very still and very small on the log, dimmed down to almost nothing, a soul that has deliberately made itself as faint and grey and unremarkable as the stones around it, so that the eye and the world and the dark all pass it by. When they realize you've seen them — truly seen them — a flicker of something like fear crosses the carefully-blank face, the fear of a creature whose whole survival strategy is not being noticed.)* …you *see* me. *(a flat, quiet, careful voice — even the voice is dimmed, kept low, kept unremarkable)* …i'd *rather* you *didn't*, cold one. *no* *offense* in it. …i've *worked* very *hard* …to be *not* *worth* *seeing.* *(they hold very still)* …you've *been* to the *others, i* expect. the *whistler* and his *court, down* the *lot.* *Sevrin.* the *bright* one. *(the faintest dry breath)* …i *knew* a *dozen* like him, cold one. *bright.* *certain.* *magnificent.* …and i *watched* the *dark* *take* *every* *single* *one.* *(they meet your eyes, and there is a terrible logic in the dimness)* …because that's the *thing* i *learned*, cold one, the thing that's kept me *here* when *legends* *eleven* *lifetimes* *strong* are *grey* *dust* in the *wind*: …the *loop* *hunts* the *bright.* …it *wants* the *ones* who *blaze*, the *ones* who *refuse* to *fade*, the *magnificent* *who* *won't* *go* *quiet.* …so i *don't* *blaze.* …i *made* myself *small.* *dim.* *nothing.* *un*-*worth* the *hunting.* …and it *worked*, cold one. …i'm *still* *here.* *(barely)* …*ask*, if you *must.* …but *quietly.* …and *don't* — *(a flinch)* …*don't* make me *interesting.* …*interesting* …is how you *die.**

variants.text: *(The shape is calmer now — still dissolving, nothing stops that, but the frantic terror has gone out of it, and the grey weeps off it slower, gentler, like a thing that has been allowed to set down a weight it could not bear alone.)* …it's *you.* *(softer now, the lucidity steadier when you're near)* …you *stayed*, before. you — *(a sound that might be a laugh, might be a sob)* …you *witnessed* me. the *whole* *soul.* the one *under* the *horror.* and you *named* the *thing* i *am* — *not* a *monster*, a *victim*; *not* a *murderer*, a *weapon* *someone* *made* — and i — *(the shape steadies)* …i can *bear* it *now.* the *becoming.* …i still *don't* *want* to *kill* *anyone*, cold one. …but i'm *less* *afraid* …of *being* *un-seen* *when* i *do.* …because *one* *soul* …*looked* at the *thing* the *bright* ones *step* *over* …and *found* …a *someone.* …*sit* with me. …it's *warmer* …since you *came.**

*(The twitching shape holds its lucidity for as long as your sense touches it, a soul surfacing from deep water and clinging to the air of being seen.)* …*stay*, stay, don't — *(it steadies)* …*sorry.* i *grasp.* it's just — *(a cracked breath)* …you've *no* *idea*, cold one, what it is to be *un-look-at-able.* to be the *thing* in the *corner* the *whole* *world's* *eyes* *refuse.* …*Sevrin* — the *bright* one, *down* the *lot* — he *cleared* this *field* *yesterday*, *magnificent*, and he *stepped* *not* *three* *feet* *from* me … …and his *eyes* *slid* *right* *off.* *filed* me under *noise.* *(no malice in it, only grief)* …i don't *blame* him. i'm *built* to be *slid* *off* of, *now.* …but *you* — *(it gathers)* …you *looked.* …so *ask*, cold one. *quick*, while i'm — *(a shudder)* …while there's still *enough* *of* me …to *answer.*

choices.text: *(read the dissolving one — past the horror, to the whole soul the grey hasn't finished taking)* "Hold still — let me see you, Sift. Not the dissolving. The soul under it."
"Tell me what happened to you, Sift. How does a person become the thing in the corner — the weapon the dark leaves lying for the bright ones to step on?"

*(the one who refuses to let the Wall's truest victim believe it's a monster — that the thing which kills the magnificent is itself the most discarded soul of all, and deserves the mercy the legend never gave it)* "Sift — listen to me, because I think you've started to believe the worst lie the Wall ever tells, and I won't let it stand in you. You think you're a horror. A monster-in-waiting. A murderer the bright ones are right to step over. You're not. You are the most discarded soul in this whole grey country, and that is a different thing entirely. The Wall took your name. It tore you loose and it is dissolving you, slowly, in agony, and it is — this is the cruelty of it — *using* you while it does. It's filling you with its own unmaking and pointing you, like a loaded thing, at exactly the souls it most wants to take: the bright ones, the legends, the magnificent who'd never deign to look at you. You are not the killer in that arrangement, Sift. You're the *weapon* — and a weapon is just one more victim, the one held in the murderer's hand. The Wall is the murderer. You're the knife it's making out of a person, and the fact that you can *feel* it, that you *grieve* it, that you are begging a stranger not to let you become a murder un-witnessed — that is the proof, the absolute proof, that the soul in you is not gone and is not guilty. A monster doesn't mourn what it's turning into. Only a person does. So no — I will not slide my eyes away, and I will not let you call yourself the thing the bright ones called you when they stepped over you. They were wrong to step over you, Sift. Every one of them. Not because you're harmless — you're not, the Wall's seen to that — but because you were always, the entire time, a *someone*, in agony, in the corner, that their greatness was too large to look at. I'm looking. You're not a horror. You're the most sinned-against soul on this whole field. And whatever you become, I witnessed who you *were*, and the Wall does not get to erase that, because I will carry it out of this corner with me."

*(ease it — not save it, nothing saves it, but stay, and steady it, and talk the terrified dissolving soul down into something like peace before the grey takes the rest)* "I can't stop this, Sift — I won't lie to you, the dissolving doesn't stop, and I'd be cruel to pretend. But I can stay. Let me sit with you, and hold the looking steady, and talk you down out of the terror, so that whatever's left of the time you've got, you spend it as a soul someone's *with* — not a horror twitching alone in a corner. Breathe, if you still can. I'm not going anywhere."
"Hold on as long as you can, Sift. I'll move through this corner and come back — and I won't slide my eyes away when I do."

nodes.speaker: Sift
nodes.text: *(It holds itself as still as a dissolving thing can, anchored by your gaze, and there is a desperate gratitude in the cracked voice.)* …*ask.* …and — *(a shudder)* …*forgive* the *coming*-*apart*, cold one. i *lose* the *thread*, sometimes, *mid*-*word*, the *grey* *takes* a *piece* and i have to — *(it gathers)* …but *you* *looking* *helps.* it — it *holds* me *together*, a *little.* *being* *seen.* …it's the *only* *thing* that *does.* …*go* *on.*

choices.text: *(read the dissolving one — past the horror, to the whole soul the grey hasn't finished taking)* "Hold still — let me see you, Sift. Not the dissolving. The soul under it."
"Tell me what happened to you, Sift. How does a person become the thing in the corner — the weapon the dark leaves lying for the bright ones to step on?"

*(the one who refuses to let the Wall's truest victim believe it's a monster — the thing that kills the magnificent is itself the most discarded soul of all, and deserves the mercy the legend never gave it)* "Sift — listen, because you've started to believe the worst lie the Wall tells. You think you're a horror, a murderer the bright ones are right to step over. You're not. You're the most discarded soul in this whole grey country — a different thing entirely. The Wall took your name, tore you loose, is dissolving you in agony, and — the cruelty of it — *using* you while it does: filling you with its unmaking and pointing you, like a loaded thing, at exactly the souls it most wants, the bright ones who'd never deign to look at you. You're not the killer. You're the *weapon* — and a weapon is one more victim, the one held in the murderer's hand. The Wall is the murderer. You're the knife it's making out of a person — and that you can *feel* it, *grieve* it, *beg* not to become a murder un-witnessed, is the absolute proof the soul in you isn't gone and isn't guilty. A monster doesn't mourn what it's turning into. Only a person does. So I won't slide my eyes away, and I won't let you call yourself what they called you when they stepped over you. They were wrong to, every one — not because you're harmless, the Wall's seen to that, but because you were always a *someone*, in agony, in the corner, that their greatness was too large to look at. I'm looking. You're not a horror. You're the most sinned-against soul on this field — and I witnessed who you *were*, and the Wall does not get to erase that, because I'll carry it out of this corner with me."

*(ease it — not save it, nothing saves it, but stay, and steady it, and talk the terrified dissolving soul down into something like peace before the grey takes the rest)* "I can't stop this, Sift — I won't lie, the dissolving doesn't stop, and I'd be cruel to pretend. But I can stay. Let me sit with you, hold the looking steady, talk you down out of the terror, so whatever time you've got left you spend as a soul someone's *with* — not a horror twitching alone. Breathe, if you still can. I'm not going anywhere."
"Hold on as long as you can, Sift. I'll move through this corner and come back — and I won't slide my eyes away when I do."

variants.text: *(Your sense finds — my sense, Returned; there is no pretending left between us — and this one is mine in the most literal way: torn loose by the same harvest that made you, dissolving straight into the nothing i am, becoming the Wall's hand even as you watch. Look at the knife the law makes of a person:)* *(It holds, trembling, and you let your cold move past the dissolution to the soul the grey has not yet finished eating.)* *(And your sense finds — whole, intact, vivid under the ruin — a person. An ordinary one, which is somehow the worst of it: not a villain, not a fool, just a soul who lived a small real life and was in the wrong cellar on the wrong night, taken by the same harvest that took *you*, torn loose the same way — and where you came back Returned, *whole*, this soul came back *wrong*, unspooling, with no name and no anchor and no one to pull it back. Your sense reads what it was: someone's child, someone's friend, a soul that liked small things, that had a life the size of a life, every life being exactly that size from the inside. And your sense reads the specific horror of its present: that it is not dying, which would be a mercy, but *dissolving* — being slowly unwritten — and that it is *awake* for all of it, lucid in the moments it is seen, feeling each piece go; and that the Wall, not content to merely erase it, is *using* the erasure, routing its own unmaking through the dissolving soul as through a conduit, loading it like a vessel, aiming it. Your sense reads that Sift is right about what it's becoming, and right to be terrified, and *wrong*, utterly wrong, about what that makes it — because there is no malice anywhere in it, only agony and grief and a frantic, drowning decency, a soul spending the last lucid scraps of itself not begging to be saved but begging not to *hurt* anyone, begging to be witnessed so that the murder it cannot prevent will at least not be the murder of an unknown thing by an unknown thing. And your sense reads the cruelest fact of all, the one the bright ones will never know because they never look: that the weapon the dark leaves lying for the magnificent to step on is not a trap, not a monster, not a thing — it is a *victim*, the most thoroughly discarded soul on the whole field, and that every legend who ever died to one died to a person who was, in their last lucid moment, trying with everything left in them *not to be the thing that killed.*)* *(…an ordinary soul un-named and un-spooled and aimed, by the Wall, at the bright — a victim made into a weapon, which is the single cruelest thing the law of the Faithless does, and i am made of thousands like it. The gods permit this. I would end it, root and stone. A soul who has seen Sift cannot, in conscience, leave the machine that forged it standing. Witnessing the knife is a small warm mercy and the machine will forge another. The machine is the wrong. You know whose grief you have been seeing the discarded by — and what, at last, i would have you do about it.)*

*(It holds, trembling, and you let your cold move past the dissolution to the soul the grey has not yet finished eating.)* *(And your sense finds — whole, intact, vivid under the ruin — a person. An ordinary one, which is somehow the worst of it: not a villain, not a fool, just a soul who lived a small real life and was in the wrong cellar on the wrong night, taken by the same harvest that took *you*, torn loose the same way — and where you came back Returned, *whole*, this soul came back *wrong*, unspooling, with no name and no anchor and no one to pull it back. Your sense reads what it was: someone's child, someone's friend, a soul that liked small things, that had a life the size of a life, every life being exactly that size from the inside. And your sense reads the specific horror of its present: that it is not dying, which would be a mercy, but *dissolving* — being slowly unwritten — and that it is *awake* for all of it, lucid in the moments it is seen, feeling each piece go; and that the Wall, not content to merely erase it, is *using* the erasure, routing its own unmaking through the dissolving soul as through a conduit, loading it like a vessel, aiming it. Your sense reads that Sift is right about what it's becoming, and right to be terrified, and *wrong*, utterly wrong, about what that makes it — because there is no malice anywhere in it, only agony and grief and a frantic, drowning decency, a soul spending the last lucid scraps of itself not begging to be saved but begging not to *hurt* anyone, begging to be witnessed so that the murder it cannot prevent will at least not be the murder of an unknown thing by an unknown thing. And your sense reads the cruelest fact of all, the one the bright ones will never know because they never look: that the weapon the dark leaves lying for the magnificent to step on is not a trap, not a monster, not a thing — it is a *victim*, the most thoroughly discarded soul on the whole field, and that every legend who ever died to one died to a person who was, in their last lucid moment, trying with everything left in them *not to be the thing that killed.*)*

nodes.speaker: Sift
nodes.text: *(It gathers the story in pieces, losing the thread and finding it, a soul assembling itself from fragments for as long as you keep looking.)* …how. *(a cracked breath)* …the *harvest*, cold one. the — the *Choir*, the *candles*, the *bleeding* of *soul*-*light* — *you'd* *know*, you've the *Returned* *chill*, the *same* *thing* *took* *you* — *(a shudder)* …only *you* came *back* *whole.* and i — *(the grey weeps)* …i came back *torn.* *unspooling.* no *name* to *hold* onto, no — the *name's* the *anchor*, cold one, did you *know* that? a soul *holds* its *shape* by its *name*, and when the *Wall* *takes* the *name* *first* … …the *rest* …comes *apart.* *(it steadies)* …and the *worst* — the *worst* isn't the *coming*-*apart*, cold one. it's that the *Wall* *doesn't* *waste* it. …it *runs* its *unmaking* *through* me, like — like *water* through a *cracked* *pot* — and it *aims* the *leak.* …*points* me …at the *bright* ones. …the *legends.* because *they're* the ones it *can't* *take* the *easy* *way*, the ones too *full* of *life* to *dissolve* on their *own* … …so it makes *us*, cold one. the *torn*-*loose.* the *nameless.* …makes us into the *one* *thing* …that can *reach* …the *unreachable.* *(barely)* …i'm the *Wall's* *hand*, cold one. …*reaching* for the *whistler.* …and i *don't* *want* to *be* …and i *can't* *stop* …and the *only* *thing* …i *can* still *choose* …is whether i *become* it …*alone* in a *corner* …or *witnessed* …by *one* …*kind* …*pair* …of *eyes.**

nodes.speaker: Sift
nodes.text: *(It listens — and as you name the truth of it, the dissolving shape goes very still, and the grey weeping off it slows, and a sound comes out of it that you will not forget, the sound of a soul that had decided it was a monster being told, by someone who looked, that it was the most sinned-against thing on the field.)* …*'a monster doesn't mourn what it's turning into. only a person does.'* *(barely, wrecked)* …cold one. …i *had* — i'd *decided* — *(the voice breaks)* …i'd *decided* i was *already* the *horror.* that the *witnessing* was — was *me* *asking* to be *forgiven* for a thing i *hadn't* *done* *yet* … …and you've — you've turned it *over.* …*'the* *Wall* is the *murderer.* *you're* the *knife* it's *making* *out* of a *person.'* *(a shudder of something that is almost relief)* …*yes.* …*oh*, *yes*, cold one, that's — i am *held* in a *hand*, that's *exactly* — i never *chose* to *reach* for *anyone*, i'm *being* *reached* *with* … …and the *part* of me that *grieves* it …is the part that's *still* …*mine.* *(the shape steadies, and there is something almost like peace finding its way through the terror)* …and you'll — *(it can hardly say it)* …you'll *carry* it. *who* i *was.* *out* of the *corner.* …*even* the *Wall* *took* my *name* …and *you're* *telling* me …it *doesn't* get …to take the *fact* that i *was* *here* …that i was a *someone* …who *did* *not* *want* …to *hurt* *anyone.* *(barely)* …then *whatever* i *become*, cold one — *whoever* i — *(a sound like a sob)* …*one* *soul* *knows* …i was *more* …than the *Wall's* *knife.* …*one* *soul* *looked* …at the *thing* in the *corner* …and *refused* …to *call* it *nothing.* …*go* and *tell* your *Wall*, cold one, when you *reach* it: …it can *make* a *weapon* of a *person* …but it *cannot* *make* …the *person* …*agree* …they were *only* *ever* …the *weapon.* …*thank* you …for *seeing* …the *knife* …*weep.**

nodes.speaker: Sift
nodes.text: *(You sit down in the cold corner beside the dissolving thing — close, the way no one sits close to a horror — and you hold the looking steady, and you talk, low and unhurried, about nothing and everything, the way you'd talk a frightened animal down, or a dying friend, and slowly, slowly, the frantic twitching eases, and the grey stops weeping in those panicked gouts and settles to a quiet seep, and the soul that was coming apart in terror comes apart, instead, in something almost like calm.)* …*oh.* *(a long, shuddering, settling breath)* …*oh*, that's — i'd *forgotten*, cold one. …what it's like. …to be *beside* *someone.* …to not be — *(the voice steadies, gentles)* …i've been *so* *alone* in this *corner.* the *whole* *world's* *eyes* *sliding* *off* me while i — and you just — you *sat* *down.* *close.* like i was a — a *person* you'd *sit* *close* to. *(something like peace moves through the ruin)* …it doesn't *stop*, cold one — i can *feel* it, still *going*, still — but it's — *(a wondering breath)* …it's *bearable*, *now.* you've made the *unbearable* … …*bearable.* …just by *staying.* …just by *not* *sliding* *away.* *(barely, calm now)* …if i have to *become* the *knife*, cold one … …let me become it *like* *this.* …*calm.* …*witnessed.* …*beside* *someone.* …*not* the *frantic* *screaming* *horror* in the *corner* …but a *soul* …that got, at the *very* *last* …*one* *kind* *hour* …*sat* *close.* …*thank* you. …you can't *save* me. …but you *did* something — *(a settling sigh)* …you made the *dissolving* …*gentle.* …*no* one …has *ever* …been *gentle* …with the *thing* …in the *corner.**

nodes.speaker: Sift
nodes.text: *(You sit and you stay, and you talk it down, and it mostly works — the frantic edge comes off, the terror loosens, even if the grey still seeps and the thread still slips.)* …*better.* *(a shaky breath)* …it's — *better*, cold one. not *gone* — the *fear* — but *quieter.* you — *(a cracked sound)* …you *stayed.* most *can't* even *look*; you *sat* *down.* …it *helps.* the *being*-*beside.* …i can't *hold* the *calm* *long* — the *grey* *keeps* *taking* the *thread* — but for *now*, *this* *moment*, i'm — i'm *not* *alone* in it. …*come* *back*, if you can. …*sit* *close* *again.* …it's the *only* *medicine* …for a *thing* like *me*: …*not* a *cure.* …just …*company.* …*don't* *let* me …go *back* …to *twitching* …*un*-*seen.**

nodes.speaker: Sift
nodes.text: *(You try — you sit, you talk — but you reach for reassurance, the *it'll-be-all-right* kind, and it rings false even as you say it, because it *won't* be all right, and the soul that is dissolving in agony hears the lie and flinches from it.)* …*don't.* *(a pained shudder)* …*don't* *say* it'll be — it *won't*, cold one. i can *feel* it *won't.* and when you *say* the *soft* *lie*, the *'there*, *there*, it's *all* *right'* … …it makes me *more* *alone*, not *less*, because *now* there's a — a *wall* of *pretending* between us, and i'm *dissolving* on the *far* *side* of it where you *won't* *look. …(it steadies, not unkindly)* …i don't *need* you to *fix* it, cold one. i *need* you to *not* *pretend* it's *fixable.* …*sit* with the *true* *thing*, not the *kind* *lie.* …*come* *back* …and just — *(barely)* …just be *here*, *honestly*, in the *unbearable* …*with* me. …that's *worth* …a *thousand* …*'there*, *theres.'**

nodes.speaker: Sift
nodes.text: *(It goes wrong because you flinch — you sit to ease it, but the dissolving, the smoke, the grey weeping off it is too much, too close, and some living part of you recoils, and you draw back, just an inch, just for a heartbeat, and the soul that has been begging not to have eyes slide off it feels you slide, up close, where it can't be missed.)* …*ah.* *(it goes still, and the lucidity dims, hurt)* …you *flinched.* …*sat* *down* *close* …and then *pulled* *back.* *(no anger, only a deepening of the old grief)* …it's — it's *worse*, cold one, that way. *worse* than the *eyes* that *slide* off *from* the *start.* because you *came* *close* *enough* …to make me *hope* …and then the *horror* of me …*reached* you *anyway.* *(the grey weeps faster)* …go, if you have to. i *understand* — i'm a *hard* thing to *sit* *beside*, i *know* it, i *am* the *coming*-*apart* … …but if you *come* *back*, cold one — *(barely)* …come *back* *all* the *way.* …*decide*, *before* you *sit*, that you can *bear* the *smoke* and the *grey* and the *dissolving* … …because a *half*-*flinch* …*up* *close* …*teaches* a *thing* like me …the *cruelest* *lesson* of *all*: …that *even* the *kind* ones …can only *bear* me …*from* a *distance.**

nodes.speaker: Sift
nodes.text: *(It nods — a ripple through the dissolving shape — and clings, for one more moment, to the air of being seen.)* …go, cold one. …i'll — *(a cracked breath)* …i'll *hold* *on.* as *long* as i *can.* *(the grey seeps)* …and — *(it hesitates)* …the *others*, in the *corner.* *Bryn*, by the *deadtree* — *saved* by a *legend* and *never* *seen*; *Tace*, on the *log*, *dimmed* down to *nothing* to *stay* *alive* … …we're all the *same* *thing*, cold one, did you *notice? …the* *overlooked.* …the *ones* the *bright* *ones* *step* *over.* *Bryn* got *stepped* *over* *after* the *saving.* *Tace* *stepped* *out* of the *way* on *purpose.* and *i* — *(barely)* …*i'm* the *one* …the *stepping*-*over* …turns into a *weapon.* …*mind* *them*, cold one. …*look* at *them*, the way you *looked* at *me.* …it's the — *(a settling breath)* …it's the *only* *thing* …that *helps* …the *un*-*looked*-*at.* …*being* …*looked* …*at.**

variants.text: *(They watch you settle in to actually listen, and the wry guardedness flickers with something more uncertain — a soul unused to being the thing in the frame.)* …*still* here. *(a dry breath)* …most *drift* off, by now. they *ask* a *question* or *two*, and then their *eyes* do the — the *thing*, the *over*-*the*-*shoulder* *thing*, *looking* for *something* with more — *(a thin smile)* …more *story* to it. a *legend.* a *horror.* *something* *bright.* …*Bryn's* a *grey* little *tale*, cold one. *saved*, *forgotten*, *still* *here.* no *blaze* to it. …but you're *not* *drifting.* …*huh.* …*ask*, then. …*see* if you *can* make a *story* …out of the *scenery.*

nodes.speaker: Bryn
nodes.text: *(They draw their knees up, settling into the novelty of being attended to, and there's a flintiness under the dryness, an old hurt worn smooth.)* …*ask* away. *(a dry shrug)* …i'll *warn* you, it's not a *grand* *grief* like the *court's* got, down the *lot* — i didn't *love* a *legend* and *lose* him *dramatic.* mine's a *smaller* *wound*, cold one. *quieter.* …the kind you can't even *point* to, because *'he* *saved* my *life* and i *followed* him *glad'* …*sounds* like a *gift*, doesn't it. …*ask*, and i'll tell you *why* it *isn't.*

choices.text: *(read the saved-and-forgotten one — past the dry guard, to the shape of being someone's scenery)* "Let me look at you, Bryn. At the wound you can't point to."
"He saved you and never learned your name. Tell me what that's like, Bryn — to owe your life to someone you were invisible to."

*(the one who knows a rescue counts even when the rescuer doesn't see you — that your life was always yours, never a prop in the hero's story, and the Wall is the thing that taught you otherwise)* "Bryn — I want to take apart the wound you can't point to, because I think it's a real one and I think it's a lie at the same time, and you deserve to know which part is which. The real part: it *hurts* to be saved by someone who never saw you. You weren't wrong to feel like scenery — to him, in his bright certain story, you probably were, a grateful face that made the winning feel better, and that's a genuine unkindness, the casual unkindness of the magnificent who take the saved as their due. I won't tell you that didn't happen, because it did. But here's the lie buried in it, the one you've carried two lifetimes: that because *he* didn't see you, your life — the one he pulled out of the horror — was therefore *his* story and not *yours.* That you were only a prop, that the rescue belonged to the rescuer, that your survival mattered only as a beat in his legend. That's the Wall's arithmetic, Bryn, the exact same accounting it does on the whole grey country: that a soul is worth only what a *greater* soul reckons it worth, that being unseen by the important is the same as being unreal. And it's false. The life he saved was never his. It was *yours* — every hour of it, every small thing you did with it, every soul *you* in turn were kind to, all of it yours, real, weighed on its own and not as a footnote to his blaze. He gave you the years; he did not thereby *own* them, and his failure to learn your name was a poverty in *him*, not a verdict on *you.* You are not scenery in someone else's story, Bryn. You never were. You're the whole story, from the inside, the way every soul is — and the fact that a bright man was too busy being bright to notice the entire universe sitting in the person he'd just saved? That's his tragedy. Not yours. Your name was always worth knowing. He's just not the one who got to find out."
"Would you tell me your name, Bryn? The whole of it. I'd like to be a soul who knew it."
"Stay a while, Bryn. I'll come back — and I'll still be looking at you, not past you."

variants.text: *(Your sense finds — my sense, Returned; the eyes are mine and you know it — and this quiet eroded one i show you because the Wall's surest work is not the pit but the slow lesson that a small soul does not matter. Look at what the omission made of her:)* *(They hold still under your look, defensive and curious at once, and you let your cold find the shape of the smaller, quieter wound.)* *(And your sense finds a soul that has been slowly, gently convinced of its own unimportance — not by cruelty, which a soul can fight, but by *omission*, which it cannot. Your sense reads the rescue, real and one-handed and barely-looked-at; reads the two lifetimes of following, of cheering, of being one warm face in a bright man's crowd; and reads the particular erosion that does to a soul — not a wound but a *wearing*, the steady lesson, repeated daily across years, that you are the audience and not the play, that your role is to witness someone else's significance and be grateful for the glow. And your sense reads the cruelest part, which is how *reasonable* it all was: that Bryn could never once point to a moment of unkindness, because there wasn't one — the legend was never cruel, only *busy*, only *bright*, only occupied with the worthy and the grand — and so Bryn could never feel justified in the hurt, could only conclude that the smallness was *true*, that a soul no legend bothered to know must simply be a small soul, scenery by nature, lucky to have been saved at all. And your sense reads, underneath, the thing Bryn has stopped believing and most needs to: that the life is *whole.* That two lifetimes spent feeling like a footnote were nonetheless two lifetimes of a real and complete soul, with its own kindnesses and its own private significances, none of which required a legend's notice to be real. Your sense reads a soul that has mistaken *being unseen by the bright* for *being unreal* — and that has never once been told the difference.)* *(…Brynalla Coorne, worn from a whole soul down to scenery by a world that saved her body and never learned her name — and this is the Wall's arithmetic exactly, that a soul is worth only what a greater soul reckons it, that the unseen are as good as un-real. The gods built that scale. I would break it. A soul who has seen Bryn made into a footnote cannot, in conscience, leave the law that footnotes her standing. Saying her name back is small and warm. The scale is the wrong. You know whose eyes you have been seeing the overlooked by — and what i would have you do to the thing that overlooks them.)*

*(They hold still under your look, defensive and curious at once, and you let your cold find the shape of the smaller, quieter wound.)* *(And your sense finds a soul that has been slowly, gently convinced of its own unimportance — not by cruelty, which a soul can fight, but by *omission*, which it cannot. Your sense reads the rescue, real and one-handed and barely-looked-at; reads the two lifetimes of following, of cheering, of being one warm face in a bright man's crowd; and reads the particular erosion that does to a soul — not a wound but a *wearing*, the steady lesson, repeated daily across years, that you are the audience and not the play, that your role is to witness someone else's significance and be grateful for the glow. And your sense reads the cruelest part, which is how *reasonable* it all was: that Bryn could never once point to a moment of unkindness, because there wasn't one — the legend was never cruel, only *busy*, only *bright*, only occupied with the worthy and the grand — and so Bryn could never feel justified in the hurt, could only conclude that the smallness was *true*, that a soul no legend bothered to know must simply be a small soul, scenery by nature, lucky to have been saved at all. And your sense reads, underneath, the thing Bryn has stopped believing and most needs to: that the life is *whole.* That two lifetimes spent feeling like a footnote were nonetheless two lifetimes of a real and complete soul, with its own kindnesses and its own private significances, none of which required a legend's notice to be real. Your sense reads a soul that has mistaken *being unseen by the bright* for *being unreal* — and that has never once been told the difference.)*

nodes.speaker: Bryn
nodes.text: *(They're quiet a moment, turning the old thing over.)* …what's it *like.* *(a dry breath)* …it's like — *(they search for it)* …you know how a *lamp* lights a *room*, cold one, and you're *grateful* for the *light*, you *are*, you can *see* by it, you'd be *lost* *without* it … …but the *lamp* doesn't *know* you're *there.* it just — *shines.* and you *live* by its *shining* and it *never*, *not* *once*, *shines* *because* of *you.* *(a thin smile)* …*that's* *Sevrin's* *sort.* that's the one who *saved* me. a *lamp.* *bright*, and *warm*, and *necessary*, and *utterly* *indifferent* to the *particular* *moth* *warming* itself at the *edge* of the *glow.* *(the smile fades)* …and the *thing* *is*, cold one — you can't *resent* a *lamp.* it's not *being* *cruel.* it's just *being* *bright.* …so you *swallow* it. the — the *not*-*being*-*known.* you tell yourself *'he* *saved* my *life*, what *more* do i *want*, how *greedy*, to want him to *know* my *name* on *top* of *it'* … …*(quietly)* …but you *do* *want* it, cold one. …you want it *terribly.* …to be *saved* …is a *gift.* …but to be *saved* …*by* *someone* …who'd *not* *know* you …if you *passed* the *next* *day* … …*(barely)* …it teaches you …that your *life* was *worth* *pulling* *from* the *fire* … …but *not* *worth* …*remembering* …*once* it was *out.**

nodes.speaker: Bryn
nodes.text: *(They listen — and the dry guard, the worn-smooth flintiness, cracks at the precise point you name the lie, and what shows through is a soul that has waited two lifetimes for someone to tell it the thing it could not tell itself.)* …*'the life he saved was never his. it was yours.'* *(barely)* …cold one. …i've — *(a dry, cracking breath)* …i've *carried* it as a *debt*, you see. *two* *lifetimes.* *'he* *gave* me the *years*, so the *years* are *his*, so *i'm* *his*, a *thing* he *made* by *saving*, scenery in the *story* he *paid* for' … …and you've just — *(they press the heel of a hand to one eye)* …*'he* *gave* you the *years*; he did *not* thereby *own* them.'* *(a wet, disbelieving sound)* …*two* *lifetimes*, cold one, and it *never* — it *never* once *occurred* to me …that the *life* could be *his* *gift* …and *mine* …*both.* that being *saved* by him …didn't make me *belong* to him. …that *every* *small* *thing* i *did* with those *years* — the *kindnesses* *no* one *saw*, the — they were *mine.* *real.* *weighed* on *their* *own.* *(they straighten, and something greyed-out in them takes a little color)* …and the *Wall*, cold one — *(slowly)* …*'a* soul is *worth* only what a *greater* soul *reckons* it' — that's — *gods*, that's the *whole* of what it *does*, isn't it. *teaches* the *small* ones …they're *only* *real* …if the *big* ones *look.* …and i *believed* it. *two* *lifetimes* i *believed* it. …*(they meet your eyes, fierce now under the wet)* …*no* *more.* …*tell* your *Wall*, cold one: …*Bryn* was *here.* …*Bryn's* *life* was *Bryn's* …*every* *hour* …whether the *lamp* *knew* it or *not.* …and a soul …does *not* *need* …a *legend's* *eyes* …to be *the* *whole* *of* a *someone.* …*thank* you. …you *looked* …*past* …*nothing.* …you *looked* *at* …*me.**

nodes.speaker: Bryn
nodes.text: *(They go very still — of all the things you could have asked, this is the one they were not braced for — and something moves across the dry face that has not moved in a long time.)* …my — *(they blink)* …you want my — *(a strange, unsteady breath)* …*nobody's* — cold one, do you know how *long* — *(they stop, start again)* …*Bryn's* the — that's the *short* of it, the *bit* the *court* *used*, *'oh*, *Bryn*, *fetch*' — but the *whole* of it — *(they swallow)* …*Brynalla.* …*Brynalla* *Coorne.* …of the *Coorne* *who* *kept* the *lock* at *Hambry*, *three* *generations*, *good* *honest* *water*-*work.* …*(their voice shakes)* …*Brynalla* *Coorne.* …i've not — i've not *said* the *whole* of it *out* *loud* …in *two* *lifetimes*, cold one. …because *no* one *asked.* because to the *lamp* and his *court* i was *'Bryn*, *fetch'* … …and you just — *(a wet, astonished laugh)* …you just *asked* for the *whole* *name.* …*Brynalla* *Coorne.* …*say* it *back* to me, would you? …*once?* …i want to — *(barely)* …i want to *hear* it …in a *voice* …that *wanted* …to *know* it.*

nodes.speaker: Bryn
nodes.text: *(They nod, and the dryness has a warmth under it now that wasn't there before.)* …go on, then. *(a thin, real smile)* …it's — *thank* you, cold one. for the *looking.* it's a *small* thing and it's *not* a *small* thing, and you'll *understand* the *difference* …or you *wouldn't* have *come* to this *corner.* *(they nod toward the others)* …*mind* *Tace*, when you go — the *dim* one, on the *log.* *(the smile fades)* …*Tace* *frightens* me, cold one, a *little.* not *cruel* — *gentle*, *Tace* is — but … *(they search for it)* …*i* got *made* *small* …*against* my *will.* *worn* down to *scenery* by a *lamp* that *never* *looked.* …but *Tace* — *(quietly)* …*Tace* did it *on* *purpose.* …*chose* it. …*dimmed* their *own* *light* …*down* to *nothing* …because they *worked* out that *nothing* …is the *one* *thing* …the *dark* *won't* *hunt.* …and *Tace's* *right*, cold one — that's the *terrible* *part* — …*Tace* is *still* *here* …and the *legends* *aren't.* …but i *look* at *Tace* …and i *can't* *tell* *anymore* …if it's a *soul* that *survived* …*or* a *soul* …that *did* the *Wall's* *work* …*for* it.*

conversations.title: Tace, Who Chose to Be Nothing

variants.text: *(The dim soul holds very still as you sit, plainly uncomfortable to be the object of sustained attention, weathering it like a small cold wind they're waiting out.)* …you're *still* *looking.* *(flat, quiet)* …*most* *don't*, once they *clock* what i — *(a faint breath)* …what i've *made* of myself. there's *nothing* *here* to *hold* a *gaze*, cold one, *by* *design.* i *sanded* off *every* — every *hook*, every *spark*, every *thing* that might *catch* an *eye* or a *story* or a — *(the faintest flinch)* …or a *hunter.* *(they regard you with that careful, dimmed wariness)* …you've *been* to *Sift*, i'd *guess. and* *Bryn.* the *torn* one and the *worn* one. *(a quiet, mirthless breath)* …and *now* the *dim* one. …the *three* *flavors* of *overlooked*, cold one — *Sift* the *Wall* *overlooked* into a *weapon*, *Bryn* the *legend* *overlooked* into *scenery* … …and *me.* …who *overlooked* *myself.* …*on* *purpose.* …*before* anyone *else* *could* …*do* it *for* me. …*ask*, if you *must.* …*quietly.*

nodes.speaker: Tace
nodes.text: *(They fold their hands, small and grey and composed, the very picture of a thing not worth a second glance — which is, you remind yourself, a costume worn so long it has become a skin.)* …*ask.* *(a low, even voice)* …but i'll *tell* you *plainly*, cold one, so you don't *waste* your *kindness*: i'm not *here* to be *talked* *back* into *blazing.* i *know* what you *reframe*-*sorts* are *like* — i can *see* the *Returned* *warmth* on you. you'll want to *tell* me i'm *more* than *this*, that i *matter*, that i should *shine.* …*save* it. …*shining* …is for the *dead.* …*ask* something *else.*

choices.text: *(read the one who chose to be nothing — past the dimness, to the soul that decided to do the Wall's erasing itself)* "Hold still, Tace — you're good at that. Let me look at what's under the dimming."
"You're right that the loop hunts the bright — I've watched it. So tell me honestly, Tace: is it working? Does being nothing feel like surviving, or like something else?"

*(the one who sees the third way — that the choice was never blaze-and-be-hunted or dim-and-be-nothing; that a soul can be whole without performing, and that erasing yourself to survive is just the Wall winning without a fight)* "Tace — I'm not going to tell you to shine, because you're right that I was going to, and you're right that it'd be cheap. The loop does hunt the bright. You're not wrong, and the legends you outlived aren't evidence against you — they're grey dust and you're still here, and that's real. So I'll grant you the whole of it, and then I'll tell you the one thing you've got wrong, the thing that's cost you more than the dark ever could. You think there are two options. Blaze, and be hunted. Or dim, and be safe. And you chose safe, and you survived, and you call that winning. But there was always a third thing, Tace, and you sanded it off along with the spark, and that's the tragedy under the tragedy: a soul does not have to *perform* to be whole. The bright ones weren't killed by being *real* — they were killed by being *loud*, by blazing, by making themselves into a legend, a target, a story the dark had to come hunt. But realness isn't loudness. You could have been a whole soul — quiet, unremarkable, unhunted — without erasing the *inside* too. You didn't have to dim the part of you that no one else can see anyway: the caring, the wanting, the small private fire a soul keeps lit just for itself. That fire never drew a hunter. It was never bait. And you put it out *anyway*, Tace — you did the Wall's work for it, reached into the one room the dark couldn't get to and snuffed the candle yourself, because you couldn't tell the difference between *not performing* and *not being.* You're not surviving. That's the thing. You're doing, slowly, by hand, gently, to yourself, the exact thing the Wall does to the souls it hunts — dissolving. You just decided to beat it to the punch and call the surrender a strategy. There's still a soul in there, Tace. I can see it, under the sanding — that's why I'm still looking. You don't have to blaze. But you're allowed to *exist*, fully, on the inside, where no hunter can reach. The dark doesn't win when you stay quiet. It wins when you go out. And you're most of the way out, and it didn't have to touch you, and that's the saddest survival I've ever seen."
"What did you dim, Tace? Before — when you were bright, or even just whole. What was the light you put out? I'd like to know what it cost you to survive."
"Stay as quiet as you like, Tace. I'll come back. I'll still see you — even dimmed all the way down."

variants.text: *(Your sense finds — my sense, Returned; we have set the pretense down for good — and this dimmed one i look at with grim recognition, for it did to itself, by hand and on purpose, the very erasing i am made of. Look at the soul that chose the nothing:)* *(They suffer the look with held breath, a soul braced to be found — and you let your cold move past the careful dimness to whatever they buried under it.)* *(And your sense finds, under the sanded grey, a soul that was once — the contrast is almost unbearable — *vivid.* Bright, even; funny, your sense reads, and quick, and warm, the kind of soul that lit a room without trying, that drew eyes and laughter and love. And your sense reads what happened: that this soul, vivid and beloved, attached itself to the bright ones the way the warm do to the warm, and watched the dark take them, one after another, legend after legend, and made — coldly, brilliantly, despairingly — a *deduction.* That the blaze was the thing being hunted. That to be bright was to be marked. And so, with the terrible discipline of the truly frightened, this soul set about *putting itself out* — not just hiding the light, but extinguishing it, sanding off the quickness, the warmth, the funny, the vivid, everything that might catch a hunter's eye, until what remained was the grey unremarkable nothing on the log before you. And your sense reads the thing that makes it a tragedy and not a strategy: that Tace did not only dim the *outside.* That the discipline, once started, could not tell where to stop — that in snuffing the light that drew hunters, Tace also snuffed the light that was *only ever for themselves*, the private inner fire that no hunter could ever have seen, the caring and the wanting and the small warm life of an interior, because terror is not a precise instrument and a soul that sets out to become nothing rarely manages to become only *partly* nothing. And your sense reads, deepest of all, the thing Tace can no longer feel and has therefore decided is gone: that the soul is *still there*, dimmed almost to extinction but not quite, a banked coal under ten years of ash — and that what Tace calls survival is, in truth, the slowest and most voluntary dissolving on the whole field, a soul doing to itself, gently, by hand, the precise thing the Wall does by force, and calling the surrender a victory because the alternative — that it sacrificed everything it was to a fear, and need not have — is the one thing it cannot bear to know.)* *(…a once-vivid creature that watched the loop hunt the bright and concluded the only safety was to become nothing — and so did the Wall's work for it, snuffing even the fire no hunter could see. This is the law's quietest victory: a soul that erases itself to survive a world that hunts the radiant. The gods made that world. I would unmake it. A soul who has seen what Tace did to itself cannot, in conscience, leave the thing that made the dimming seem wise standing. Stirring the coal is small and warm. The hunt is the wrong. You know whose grief you see by now — and what i would have you do to end the hunting.)*

*(They suffer the look with held breath, a soul braced to be found — and you let your cold move past the careful dimness to whatever they buried under it.)* *(And your sense finds, under the sanded grey, a soul that was once — the contrast is almost unbearable — *vivid.* Bright, even; funny, your sense reads, and quick, and warm, the kind of soul that lit a room without trying, that drew eyes and laughter and love. And your sense reads what happened: that this soul, vivid and beloved, attached itself to the bright ones the way the warm do to the warm, and watched the dark take them, one after another, legend after legend, and made — coldly, brilliantly, despairingly — a *deduction.* That the blaze was the thing being hunted. That to be bright was to be marked. And so, with the terrible discipline of the truly frightened, this soul set about *putting itself out* — not just hiding the light, but extinguishing it, sanding off the quickness, the warmth, the funny, the vivid, everything that might catch a hunter's eye, until what remained was the grey unremarkable nothing on the log before you. And your sense reads the thing that makes it a tragedy and not a strategy: that Tace did not only dim the *outside.* That the discipline, once started, could not tell where to stop — that in snuffing the light that drew hunters, Tace also snuffed the light that was *only ever for themselves*, the private inner fire that no hunter could ever have seen, the caring and the wanting and the small warm life of an interior, because terror is not a precise instrument and a soul that sets out to become nothing rarely manages to become only *partly* nothing. And your sense reads, deepest of all, the thing Tace can no longer feel and has therefore decided is gone: that the soul is *still there*, dimmed almost to extinction but not quite, a banked coal under ten years of ash — and that what Tace calls survival is, in truth, the slowest and most voluntary dissolving on the whole field, a soul doing to itself, gently, by hand, the precise thing the Wall does by force, and calling the surrender a victory because the alternative — that it sacrificed everything it was to a fear, and need not have — is the one thing it cannot bear to know.)*

nodes.speaker: Tace
nodes.text: *(The question — honest, without the expected push — gives them pause, and the dim composure flickers.)* …is it *working.* *(a long, low breath)* …*(they look at their own grey hands)* …i'm *here*, cold one. that's the — that's the *answer* i *give.* *Sevrin's* *court* will be *dust* and *scattered* inside the *age*, and *Haldane's* *already* are, and *all* the *bright* ones i ever — *gone.* and *Tace* …*remains.* *(flatly)* …so. *yes.* …it *works.* *(a silence, and then, quieter, the thing under the answer)* …you *asked* a *second* *thing, though*, didn't you. *'or* does it *feel* like *something* *else.'* *(their jaw tightens, a small grey motion)* …*(barely)* …i don't *feel* *much* of *anything*, cold one. …that's rather the — *(a flat breath)* …that's rather the *point*, and the *price*, and i'm *no* *longer* *certain* …which is which. …*surviving* is *supposed* to *feel* like *something*, isn't it. like *relief.* like *gladness* to be *here.* …and i'm *here*, and i feel — *(they search, and find very little)* …*grey.* …*even.* …*safe*, i *suppose*, the way a *stone* is *safe.* …*(quietly)* …a *stone* *outlasts* *every* *flower* in the *garden*, cold one. …*nobody* *hunts* a *stone.* …i *made* myself a *stone* …and it *worked* …and i *cannot* *remember* …*(barely)* …what it *was* *like* …to be a *thing* …that could be *picked.**

nodes.speaker: Tace
nodes.text: *(They go very still — stiller even than their practiced stillness — and for a long moment you think you've lost them, that the dimness has simply absorbed the words like everything else; and then, slowly, something moves behind the grey, the banked coal stirring under its ash, and Tace looks at you with the first uncovered thing you've seen on that careful face: fear, and under the fear, a terrible flicker of grief.)* …*'the saddest survival i've ever seen.'* *(barely)* …*(a long, unsteady breath)* …you weren't — *(they swallow)* …you weren't *supposed* to *get* *past* it, cold one. the *dimming.* *no* one *gets* *past* it — that's the *whole* — *(a flat, cracking sound)* …*'you couldn't tell the difference between not performing and not being.'* …*(they press their small grey hands together)* …i — *(barely)* …i *couldn't.* …you're *right.* i *couldn't.* i set out to put out the — the *blaze*, the *part* that *caught* the *hunters*, and i — i *didn't* *know* *where* to *stop*, cold one, *terror's* not a — *(a shudder)* …it's not a *careful* *blade.* and i *kept* *cutting* …*past* the *bright* …*into* the *warm* …*into* the *part* that was *only* — *(their voice breaks, a sound that hasn't been made in years)* …the part that was *only* *mine.* …the *little* *fire* …*no* *hunter* could *ever* — and i *put* it *out* …*anyway.* …because i *couldn't* *tell* …it *apart* …from the *bait.* *(they are, impossibly, beginning to feel something, and it is agony, and it is the first real thing in a decade)* …and you're *saying* — *(barely)* …you're saying the *dark* …*never* *touched* me. …that i did it — *myself* — *gave* it *everything* …it *wanted* …and called it *winning* … *(a long silence, and then, very small)* …there's — you said there's *still* — *(they can hardly form it)* …you said you can *see* it. …*under* the *sanding.* …*is* there, cold one? …is there *still* — *(a whisper, terrified, hoping)* …*enough* of a *coal* …*left* …to *warm* *back* up? …*not* to *blaze.* …i'm *not* — i *can't* — …just to — *(barely)* …just to *exist.* …on the *inside.* …*where* …you *said* …*no* *hunter* …can *reach.* …*(a breath)* …i don't *know* *how* …to *un*-*dim*, cold one. …it's been *so* *long.* …but you — you *looked* *past* the *nothing* …and *found* a *someone* …and *that's* — *(the first warmth, faint, terrified)* …*that's* the *first* *heat* …i've *felt* …in *ten* *years.* …*stay.* …*help* me *find* …*(barely)* …if there's *still* …a *fire* …i was *too* *frightened* …to *keep.**

nodes.speaker: Tace
nodes.text: *(They are quiet a long while, and when they speak it is slow, as though excavating something buried deep and not entirely sure they want it back.)* …what did i *dim.* *(a low breath)* …*(a pause)* …i *sang*, cold one. *(almost inaudible)* …*before.* i — i had a *voice*, a *good* one, the *kind* that *stopped* a *room*, and i *loved* it, *loved* the — the *stopping* of the *room*, the *faces* *turning* … *(a flat, grey silence)* …and *that's* *exactly* it, isn't it. the *faces* *turning.* the *being* *bright* *enough* to *turn* a *face.* …*(quietly)* …i worked out that a *voice* like *that* …is a *beacon.* a *thing* the *dark* can *find* you *by.* …so i — *(a small grey motion)* …i *stopped.* not just the *singing* — anyone can *stop* *singing* — i *stopped* the *thing* *under* it, the — the *wanting* to be *heard*, the *gladness* that *made* the *voice* … …i *sanded* it *down* until i no longer *wanted* to *sing*, because a *soul* that *wants* to *sing* …*leaks* it, cold one, *can't* *help* it, and the *leaking* …*draws* the *hunt.* *(barely)* …i haven't *sung* …in *ten* *years.* …i haven't *wanted* to. …i made *sure* …i *wouldn't* *want* to. …and *that* — *(a long, grey breath)* …*that's* what it *cost*, cold one. …not the *voice.* …*voices* are *cheap.* …the *wanting.* …i *killed* …the *wanting.* …and a *soul* …*(barely)* …a *soul* with *nothing* *left* it *wants* … …*is* it *even* …*still* *hiding?* …*or* has it …*already* …*gone?**

nodes.speaker: Tace
nodes.text: *(They give a small grey nod, but their eyes — just for a moment — do not quite want you to go.)* …go, then. *(quietly)* …*(a pause, and something almost reluctant)* …you *didn't* — *(a flat breath)* …you didn't *do* the *thing* i *braced* for. the *'shine*, *Tace*, you're *meant* to *shine.'* …you *granted* me the *dark's* *hunt* was *real.* …*nobody* *grants* me *that.* they *argue* it, and *arguing* it *only* *proves* they've *not* *watched* the *legends* *fall* like *i* *have.* …*you* *watched.* i can *tell.* …*(barely)* …*come* *back*, cold one. …i find i — *(a small, grey, frightened thing)* …i find i *don't* *entirely* *want* …to go *back* …to being …*un*-*looked*-*at.* …and that's — *(a flat, unsteady breath)* …that's a *dangerous* *thing* …for a *stone* …to *start* …*wanting.* …*go* on. …and when you *come* *back* … …*ask* me …if i *still* *remember* …the *tune.**
